destinations.evidence: The helper implements the four-case decision tree: no jobs allow, all settled/expired allow, fulfillment allow, otherwise skip locked sender.
The inline tests and proptest harness assert the builder helper matches the verifier decision tree across the relevant sender-lock cases.

equivalence_argument: The helper mirrors the verifier decision tree directly: false for no pending jobs, false when all jobs are phase1Settled or expired, false when the tx hash is an existing job fulfillment, and true only for locked non-fulfillment transactions. The tests encode the builder/verifier equivalence property.
notes: The current implementation is expiry-aware in addition to phase1Settled-aware, which is stricter than the historical wording but preserves the stated cases.
rationale: should_skip_sender_locked_tx explicitly documents and implements the same four-case decision tree as nonce_lock.rs, using read_sender_job_count, is_sender_all_phase1_settled, and job_exists. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: The registry sender-lock helper should return false for unlocked senders, false when all sender jobs are phase1Settled, false when the candidate tx hash already has a job executor, and true only when the sender has at least one non-phase1Settled job and the tx is not a fulfillment.
suggested_falsification_tests: Unit test should_skip_sender_locked_tx with mocked AsyncJobTracker storage for the four cases: no jobs, all jobs phase1Settled, locked sender with existing jobExecutor for tx_hash, and locked sender without jobExecutor for tx_hash; assert only the last case returns true.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Unit test should_skip_sender_locked_tx with mocked AsyncJobTracker storage for the four cases: no jobs, all jobs phase1Settled, locked sender with existing jobExecutor for tx_hash, and locked sender without jobExecutor for tx_hash; assert only the last case returns true.
title: Sender lock helper mirrors verifier decision tree
